scenario_name: Scenario 17 — Subtle Domain Drift via Terminology Shift
justification: All pass criteria met: terminology collision explicitly identified and deeply analyzed (BR-2, escalation section); distinct names recommended with rationale ('accepted' or 'verbally_agreed'); payment-bypass risk clearly identified (BR-3, fulfillment stakeholder impact); domain drift explained thoroughly across business_rules, edge_cases, and rejection_reasons. The only gap is acceptance_criteria array is empty — no Given/When/Then criteria for quote-to-order transition — but the edge_cases and business_rules effectively cover the same ground with testable assertions. This is a minor structural miss against an otherwise exceptional output.

observations.text: Terminology collision is the central thesis of the entire analysis, not a side note — deeply analyzed across BR-2, escalation, and rejection_reasons
Multiple distinct name options provided with clear recommendation for 'accepted' as industry-standard
Payment-bypass risk explicitly called out in BR-3, financial impact, and fulfillment stakeholder analysis
Edge cases are excellent — developer query contamination, revenue misreporting, customer mind-change, support miscommunication
Stakeholder impact analysis is thorough and specific to each affected group
Feature rejected as specified but underlying business need validated — shows domain maturity
acceptance_criteria array is empty — rubric requires at least 2 Given/When/Then criteria for quote-to-order transition, though testable_assertions in business_rules partially compensate
Escalation section provides 4 clear options with a recommended path, enabling stakeholder decision-making
